behavior_summary: Handles egg state transitions and hatching behavior for a GHOSTEGG item.

U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea (Event 10) runs when the egg item leaves a fast-area trigger: it reads the egg's eggId and, if eggId < 0x64, subtracts 0x64 and writes the result back; if eggId >= 0x64 it returns without change. This appears to clear/unset a previously applied 0x64 state flag.

U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch (Event 7, helper) performs the hatching sequence: it aborts if eggId >= 0x64, otherwise for eggId == 1 it applies a probabilistic gate via urandom(10); when proceeding it sets eggId to 0x64, creates an NPC (type 0x23), marks it with a status bit, places it at the egg's coordinates, spawns an asynchronous NPC behavior, assigns a target and alignment, and forces it into combat.

eggId values are used as an encoded state: values >= 0x64 mark a 'hatched/in-progress/flagged' state; U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea reverses a 0x64 addition in some cases so state can change when the item leaves the trigger region.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea is triggered by area-exit; hatch is triggered by a hatch event (separate event handler).
State gate: both U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea and hatch check eggId against 0x64 to guard duplicate or re-entrant hatching logic.
Probabilistic branch: U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch uses urandom(10) when eggId == 1 to sometimes abort/delay hatching (inferred ~90% chance to proceed).
Creation sequence in hatch is multi-step and aborts early if eggId indicates already-processed state (>=0x64).
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning of eggId numeric values (what do values 0,1,n represent prior to adding 0x64?).
Who/what sets eggId prior to hatch being invoked and under which game conditions eggId == 1 vs other values occurs?
Exact meaning of the 0x64 constant (is it a bitflag, sentinel value, or index offset?) — inference is that it marks 'in-progress/hatched' state.
Which target pid is set on the spawned NPC (Npc::setTarget) — player, nearby NPC, or the egg's owner? The disassembly does not make the target source explicit.
Exact status bit mask applied with Item::orStatus on the newly created NPC (what gameplay flag is being set?).

main_function.name: U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: On area-exit, check and adjust the egg's eggId state to clear a previously-applied 0x64 flag when appropriate so the egg's state can transition after leaving the trigger region.
entry_conditions: Event 10 (U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea) — triggered when the egg item leaves its fast-area trigger region
Parameter: egg_item (the item instance triggering the event) provided by the engine

function_map.name: U8P_GHOSTEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: area-exit trigger that inspects and adjusts eggId state (clears/additional-state removal)

function_map.name: U8P_GHOSTEGG::hatch
function_map.role: hatching sequence that may spawn and initialize a new NPC and set eggId to the flagged value 0x64 to prevent duplicate hatching

function_map.name: Npc::create
function_map.role: allocate/create an NPC instance of a given type

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: place/spawn an item/NPC into the world at X,Y,Z

external_calls.purpose: Retrieve current eggId/state to decide if 0x64 subtraction is needed
Store adjusted eggId (eggId - 0x64) when leaving the fast area and eggId < 0x64
Check current eggId to guard against re-hatching when eggId >= 0x64
Provide a random roll (urandom(10)) to probabilistically gate hatching when eggId == 1
Set eggId to 0x64 to mark that hatching/hatched state is in effect
Create the NPC instance that emerges from the egg (type observed as 0x23)
Apply a status bit/mask to the newly created NPC (mark the NPC with a flag)
Obtain egg X coordinate to place the spawned NPC
Obtain egg Y coordinate to place the spawned NPC
Obtain egg Z coordinate to place the spawned NPC
Place the created NPC into the world at the egg's coordinates
Compute a facing/direction for spawn behavior relative to the egg
Spawn an asynchronous behavior/movement method for the created NPC (METHOD::15E7) with direction and pid
Assign a combat/AI target to the spawned NPC
Set the spawned NPC's alignment/faction (e.g., hostile)
Mark the spawned NPC as in combat to begin combat behavior

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles egg state transitions and hatching behavior for a GHOSTEGG item.
